author: Landlines
text:
delivered_in_resolved: transparent cross-repo resolution via delivered_repo

bl-7523 wrote delivered_repo alongside delivered_in on every fresh delivery so a reader could navigate cross-repo. The write side landed; the read side did not. On a bare task hub, on a sibling client, or anywhere else the local clone's main does not carry the squash sha, delivery::resolve returned an empty Delivery and delivered_in_resolved was null even when the provenance was sitting right there on the task.

Resolution now layers in three steps. local_resolve keeps the existing hint-then-tag-scan path against the local integration branch, byte-identical for single-repo callers. A new resolve_with(opts: ResolveOpts) opts callers into a remote fallback: on a local miss with delivered_repo set, delivery_remote materializes a balls-owned bare cache at .balls/code-refs/<sha1-of-url>.git, fetches with --filter=blob:none for commit metadata only, and re-runs the [bl-id] tag scan against the fetched history. Whichever branch wins also stamps a new resolved_repo field on the Delivery, surfaced as delivered_in_resolved_repo in bl show --json so tooling always knows which repo the sha came from -- local hits report repo_url::current, remote hits report the delivered_repo URL we resolved through.

Remote resolution is opt-in. bl show grows --resolve-remote because fetching arbitrary forge URLs on every show would be rude, and the soft-fail discipline matches bl-ffb4 rather than the hub-side hard-fail of bl-dcd3: a first-time fetch failure tears the half-built cache down and warns once, a stale-cache refresh failure keeps serving warm refs with a note. A bogus delivered_repo never breaks the command -- sha just stays None.
